data.description:
## Story G: Update project docs to reflect polyglot test-gate config layer

### Description
**What**: Update CLAUDE.md quick-reference and architecture sections, CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md polyglot-gate table, KNOWN-ISSUES.md (monorepo single-winner limitation), and relevant ADRs to reflect the polyglot test-gate config layer that landed in Stories A-F.
**Why**: Future agents reading CLAUDE.md or CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md need accurate awareness of the new config keys, auto-detection behavior, and the deleted pyproject.toml precondition.
**Scope**:
- IN: updates to existing docs only (CLAUDE.md, CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md, INSTALL.md, KNOWN-ISSUES.md, ADRs)
- OUT: new doc files, new documentation patterns, tutorial content

### Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md Architecture section references the new config keys in its polyglot-gate summary
  ← Satisfies: downstream traceability (not an explicit epic SC, but required per preplanning doc-update convention)
- When this story is complete, a tree-wide grep for literal `python3 -m pytest` in plugin docs (excluding CHANGELOG and historical commits) returns zero live references
  ← Satisfies: documentation consistency

### Considerations
- [Maintainability] Follow `.claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md` for formatting and structure
## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: Update project docs to reflect polyglot test-gate config layer
